sourceText: If you know the enemy and know yourself, you need not fear the result of a hundred battles. Self-knowledge is the monumental foundation of all strategy; look in the mirror first.

Sovereignty of Self-Knowledge: Intelligence begins at home. A giant understands their internal state is the most critical variable in any conflict; achieving monumental clarity on your own capabilities and biases eliminates half the risk of any endeavor. Do not focus on the opponent until you have mastered yourself.
The supreme art of war is to subdue the enemy without fighting. Victory through intellect, not the sword, is true mastery.
Conflict is costly and unpredictable. True mastery lies in achieving victory without direct confrontation, preserving resources for all involved. Aim for bloodless triumph.
Military tactics are like water; water in its natural course avoids the high and hastens to the low. Be fluid. Adapt to the monumental change of terrain.

Rigidity precedes breaking. A giant understands the world is in constant, monumental flux; by maintaining a spirit as fluid and unstoppable as water, you find the path of least resistance to your goal. Shape your strategy to fit reality, not your ego.

Enduring the monumental frustration of his greatest work being ignored by his contemporaries and the absolute social stigma of being an open skeptic in a religious age. Facing the challenge of lifelong professional exclusion while his mind was reshaping the foundations of human thought.

Transcended the dogmas of his era to provide a new map for human understanding, proving that the mind can thrive without absolute certainty. Established a monumental standard for intellectual honesty, showing that a giant’s impact is found in their ability to face the truth without fear.
Reason is, and ought only to be the slave of the passions. Logic is a tool, but desire is the monumental engine of life. Align your heart with your head.

On the Sovereignty of Passion: Logic doesn't move the world; desire does. A giant understands their monumental mission must be fueled by deep inner conviction. By using your reason to refine and execute your passions rather than suppress them, you achieve a level of drive pure intellect cannot match. Find your fire.
A wise man proportions his belief to the evidence. Objectivity is a monumental shield against deception; trust the data of your life.

Certainty is a trap for narrow minds. A giant understands their monumental impact relies on precision, so constantly test your beliefs against experience. Stay flexible and powerful by mastering facts.
Be a philosopher; but, amidst all your philosophy, be still a man. A monumental intellect is hollow without a human touch. Stay rooted in the real world.
